ItemShare for Elder Scrolls Online

ItemShare is an ESO addon that lets players mark tradeable items as shareable in-game, save that data to saved variables, and then import it into a Google Sheet for guild-wide or group-wide request tracking.

ESO Addon Google Sheets Workflow Requires LibCustomMenu Account-wide saved variables

What the Addon Does

ItemShare adds right-click menu options to supported inventory entries so you can mark items as available to others. Once marked, the addon shows a share icon in-game, keeps the item in a shared list, and stores the data for later import into a Google Sheet.

  • Marks items as shareable from the inventory context menu
  • Blocks items that are already bound
  • Blocks items that are only temporarily tradeable, such as group trade windows from dungeons or trials
  • Shows a visual shared-item icon in inventory lists
  • Maintains a dedicated Shared Items window
  • Saves all shared entries to account-wide saved variables
  • Supports a companion Google Sheets script that imports and formats the results
  • Supports sharing a spreadsheet link directly from the addon UI
Only items that are actually eligible to be shared can be added. If an item is bound to you, or restricted to temporary group trading, the addon will not present the share option.

Requirements

  • LibCustomMenu is required for the right-click context menu integration.
  • The addon uses the saved variable file ItemShareSavedVars.
  • The companion spreadsheet workflow expects the Google Sheets script files to be included in the GoogleSheets subdirectory of the ZIP package.

Google Sheets Template & Sample Data

Use a Pre-Built Template:
You do not need to manually build the spreadsheet from scratch. A ready-to-use template is available:

Open ItemShare Template Spreadsheet

Click Use Template to create your own copy with the required sheets, formatting, and workflow already set up.
Sample Data:
The addon package includes a SampleData directory with example SavedVariables files.

These can be uploaded into the spreadsheet to test the import process, see how items are displayed, and verify script behavior before using real data.

Supported Locations

ItemShare can track shared items from multiple storage locations. The location is preserved and exported with the item.

  • Inventory
  • Bank
  • Craft Bag
  • House Storage
  • Guild Bank (functionality currently disabled while under development)
  • Furnishing Vault
  • Placed furnishings, with special handling during sync

Commands

CommandDescription
/itemshareShows command help (debug output).
/itemshare listOpens or closes the Shared Items window.
/itemshare resetClears all saved shared entries.
/itemshare syncReconciles saved entries with items you still own and updates counts and locations.
/itemshare savePrompts to reload the UI so saved variable data is written out.
/itemshare debugToggles debug messages on or off.
/itemshare guildbankToggles guild bank sharing functionality (currently disabled while under development).
/itemshare sheetDisplays the currently configured spreadsheet URL. If a URL is provided, it will be saved.
/itemshare sheet <url>Sets or updates the spreadsheet URL used for sharing.
/itemshare sheet clearClears the stored spreadsheet URL.
/itemshare sharelinkPlaces the configured spreadsheet URL into the chat input for easy sharing.

Alias: /ishare

Spreadsheet Link Integration

ItemShare allows you to store a reference to your Google Sheets document directly in-game. This makes it easier to share access to your item list with others.

  • You can store your spreadsheet URL using /itemshare sheet <url>
  • The URL will be displayed at the top of the Shared Items window
  • A button in the window allows you to send the URL to chat
  • /itemshare sharelink will prefill the chat box with the URL for easy sharing
The spreadsheet link is provided as plain text. ESO does not support opening external web links directly from the game client.

In-Game User Interface

After an item has been marked, the addon adds a small share icon to the inventory row. Hovering that icon shows a tooltip that confirms the item is available to others.

A share icon appears beside marked items in the inventory list.
Hovering the icon shows a tooltip that identifies the item as a shared item.

Context Menu Actions

Right-clicking an eligible item adds an Add to Share option. If the item is already tracked, the menu changes to Remove from Shared List.

Right-click menu showing the Add to Share action for an eligible item.
Once tracked, the same item can be removed with Remove from Shared List.

Shared Items Window

The addon also includes a dedicated Shared Items window that displays every tracked entry, including the item name, count, and location. The window position and open/closed state are saved and restored.

The in-game Shared Items window provides a quick overview of all currently tracked entries.

How Saving Works

ESO only writes saved variables at the appropriate time, so after marking items you should run /ishare save or reload the UI. The addon stores the data in your saved variables file, which can then be processed by the spreadsheet updater.

  1. Mark items in-game using Add to Share.
  2. Run /ishare save or reload the UI.
  3. Locate your saved variables file.
  4. Import that file through the Google Sheets updater.
The saved variables file is typically located in the ESO SavedVariables folder and is named ItemShare.lua or uses the saved variable data for ItemShare. Your spreadsheet updater reads that file and builds the sheets from it.

Spreadsheet Output

The companion Apps Script reads the saved variable data, creates or updates per-account sheets, and rebuilds a master sheet that combines all item sources. The importer keeps the output organized and ready for users to request items.

Per-Account Sheet

Each source account can have its own worksheet showing the items that account has offered. This view includes location and requested-by tracking.

Example per-account sheet showing item metadata, count, location, and RequestedBy.

Master Sheet

The master sheet combines all imported items into a single searchable list. This is typically the main sheet requesters will browse first.

Example master sheet that merges imported items and preserves requested-by information.

Typical Workflow

  1. Install the addon and LibCustomMenu.
  2. Mark eligible items in-game as shareable.
  3. Use /ishare save to write the latest data.
  4. Open the Google Sheet that uses the companion Apps Script.
  5. Upload the saved variables file through the spreadsheet updater.
  6. Use the master sheet or account sheet to request items.
  7. Fulfill requests and remove completed items from the sheet as needed.

Notes and Limitations

  • Bound items cannot be shared.
  • BoP group-trade items are intentionally blocked.
  • Guild bank operations are permission-aware.
  • Placed furnishings receive special sync handling so they are not removed incorrectly.
  • The spreadsheet side is a separate workflow and must be configured before first use.
Generated documentation for the ItemShare addon package.
